programOutcomes.description: Engineering knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems.

Problem analysis: Identity, formulate, research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using the first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences.

Design/development of solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health and safety, and cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.

Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions.

Modern tool usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations.

The engineer and society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice.

Environment and sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development.
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice.
Individual and teamwork: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings.

Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions.

Project management and finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.
Life-long learning: Recognize the need for and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.
Ability to design and engineer, innovative, optimal and elegant computing solutions to interdisciplinary problems using standard practices, tools and technologies.
Ability to learn emerging computing paradigms for research and innovation.

courseOutcomes.description: Understand the typical programming constructs: data (primitive and compound), control, modularity, recursion, etc. thereby to understand a given program.
Analyze the given program by tracing, identify coding errors and debug them.
Apply the programming constructs appropriately and effectively while developing computer program.
Develop computer programs that implement suitable algorithms for problem scenarios and applications.

lecturePlan.topic: Review of Physical Computing
Understanding Arduino Hardware and Software Architecture
Verifying Hardware and Software - Loading and Running your First Program
Introduction to C - Structure of C programs - Data types
Constants, Variables, Identifiers, Keywords, Declarations, Expressions, Statements, and Symbolic constants
Input and Output: getchar, putchar, scanf, printf, gets, puts, functions
lecturePlan.inClassActivity: Problem based learning using direct instruction method

lecturePlan.topic: Pre-processor commands, Preparing and running a complete C program.
Supplementary Programs of class 11 to 17
Operators and expressions: Arithmetic, unary, logical, bit-wise, assignment and conditional operators
Control statements: if-else, switch, break, continue
lecturePlan.inClassActivity: Problem based learning using direct instruction method

lecturePlan.topic: Supplementary Programs of class 24 to 25
Activity Sheet: 01
while, do-while, for statements, nested loops, goto statements, comma operator.
lecturePlan.inClassActivity: Problem based learning using direct instruction method

lecturePlan.topic: Supplementary Programs: loop statements
Activity Sheet: 02
Solve problems from R4
Mid term - Lab Assessment
Arrays: Defining and processing an array, Passing array to a function, multi-dimensional arrays, Sequential search, Sorting arrays
lecturePlan.inClassActivity: Problem based learning using direct instruction method

lecturePlan.topic: Activity Sheet: 03
Functions: Defining and accessing function, passing arguments, function prototypes
lecturePlan.inClassActivity: Problem based learning using direct instruction method

lecturePlan.topic: Storage classes
Solve problems from R3,R4
Recursion
lecturePlan.inClassActivity: Problem based learning using direct instruction method

lecturePlan.topic: Activity Sheet: 04
Pointers: Introduction, Declarations, Passing pointer to a function, Operations on pointers, Arrays and Pointers
lecturePlan.inClassActivity: Problem based learning using direct instruction method

lecturePlan.topic: Pointer to functions, Dynamic memory allocation.
Activity Sheet: 05
Structures unions & data storage: Defining and processing a structure, passing structure to a function, Pointers; and Unions
Strings: fixed length and variable length strings, strings and characters
String handling, Operations on strings String Manipulation functions
Activity Sheet :06
Files and Streams Open, Close, Create, File operations, Unformatted data files, Experiments on String Handling (Ex.no.8)
C Pre-processor - Command line arguments
Activity Sheet: 07
Developing applications using Arduino Part I & Part II
